sources_exist.note: Six of seven canonical URLs returned accessible content, but the CPA Journal owner-age page yielded zero readable lines and is not auditable under the validator standard.

figures_match_sources.note: Thomson Reuters reports 21% adoption for tax firms, not tax/audit/accounting firms as a whole; Accounting Today gives no Citrin valuation or EBITDA multiple; E-file.com does not support the cloud-accounting analog; and SmartVault does not report a 54% fixed-fee/46% hourly firm mix.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The bounded role fractions and CPA licensure rationale are broadly plausible, but t50_years=5 applies tax-firm adoption to all CPA offices, pi_dist=0.55 depends on an unsupported billing mix, and the age/succession inputs cannot be verified. These inputs are score-affecting in a record only 0.016 above the conditional cut, so the BORDERLINE flag remains unresolved.

rubric_consistent.note: All deterministic dataset inputs match exactly, and the ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag on B and S is a benign stored-precision matter. However, adoption timing and capture do not meet the rubric's population and pricing-evidence requirements, while the succession bonus is based on uncited or inaccessible claims.

cross_checks_honest.note: Uplift, terminal value, and no-proxy discussions are internally coherent, but pricing-model deflation presents the unsupported 54% fixed/46% hourly mix as fact, and the raw record contains duplicate terminal_value.justification keys, leaving only the last one effective in standard JSON parsing.

source_audits.note: The tracker reports more than 50 PE sponsors active since 2024, names repeat sponsor-backed platforms and transaction counts, and documents numerous 2025-2026 add-ons, supporting that at least 18 active consolidators is a defensible conservative count.
The page explicitly reports 4-7x normalized EBITDA for PE-platform accounting deals and 4.5-5.5x for $1M-$3M EBITDA tuck-ins, supporting the central 5.0x normalized buy multiple for the target band.

The source says enterprise GenAI adoption among tax firms rose from 8% in 2024 to 21% in 2025. It does not report 21% for tax, audit and accounting firms collectively or establish the share of all NAICS 541211 firms materially using AI.

The article concerns EisnerAmper's TowerBrook continuation vehicle and notes Blackstone's majority investment in Citrin Cooperman, but it gives no $2.0B enterprise value, EBITDA, 15x or 11x multiple, or 8-12x platform-exit range.

The page supports an IRS e-file pilot in 1986 and crossing 50% of returns in 2005. It does not support the asserted 10-12-year tax-software or cloud-accounting adoption analog, QuickBooks Online reaching about 50% accountant use by 2015-2016, or CPA-firm adoption specifically.
The page returned no readable content, so the firm-size age percentages, the 25% central estimate, and the claimed documented succession shortage could not be verified.

The guide supports that 79% of firms moved away from hourly bookkeeping and discusses fixed, value, and subscription pricing. It does not report that 54% of firms predominantly use fixed fees, that this rose from 50% in 2024, or that the remaining 46% of total CPA-firm fees are hourly or hours-anchored.

reasons: Re-run inputs.current_adoption_pct and inputs.t50_years using a source whose population covers CPA offices and whose metric is material firm use across tax, audit, and accounting; the cited Thomson Reuters page reports 21% only for tax firms.

Replace inputs.exit_mult with a disclosed $10M+ EBITDA accounting-platform transaction or a directly evidenced comparable; https://www.accountingtoday.com/news/eisneramper-completes-continuation-vehicle-with-towerbrook-capital contains none of the quoted enterprise values or multiples.

Re-run inputs.historical_analogs with citations for the organic tax-software/cloud-accounting series or restrict the derivation to the supported 1986-2005 IRS e-file series while acknowledging its mandate and transaction-level population.

Replace inputs.owners_60plus_pct and the succession_shortage_documented evidence with accessible, industry-specific citations for both the age percentages and shortage; the CPA Journal page was not readable and the bonus materially affects B.

Source the 54% fixed-fee/46% hourly-or-anchored billing mix used in inputs.pi_dist, inputs.pricing_structure, and cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation; SmartVault supports a bookkeeping shift but not the all-firm revenue split.

Reassess the BORDERLINE result after correcting the score-affecting adoption, owner/succession, exit-multiple, and pricing inputs; do not rely on the current S=4.01627 while these inputs remain unsupported.
Emit only one cross_checks.terminal_value.justification key in the re-run so the intended terminal analysis is unambiguous under standard JSON parsing.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, S
